county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Danville. Formed 1842 from Mercer and Lincoln. Danville was site of Kentucky's constitutional conventions (1792). Centre College (1819) archives hold regional materials. Boyle County Historical Society. Constitution Square State Historic Site. McDowell House museum. Perryville Civil War battlefield nearby. Check Mercer County for pre-1842 records — Mercer holds many early Bluegrass records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1842 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1842. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Boyle County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1842.

Tax lists naming slaveholders and number of enslaved; estate inventories naming enslaved individuals; slave schedules (1850, 1860); manumission records; bills of sale. Kentucky was a slave state that did not secede.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax lists and estate records generally well preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Kentucky was a border state — both Union and Confederate service records. Union pension files at NARA; Confederate pension files at KDLA. Compiled Military Service Records for both sides. USCT (United States Colored Troops) records for African American soldiers.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Brooksville. Formed 1796 from Campbell and Mason. Northern Kentucky Ohio River county. Augusta (river town) has well-preserved early records. Augusta College (1822-1849) records at KDLA. Bracken County Historical Society. Tobacco farming heritage. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Campbell and Mason counties for pre-1796 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1796 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1796. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

source_profiles.source_name: Bracken County Will and Estate Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Will books, estate inventories, settlements, and appraisements from 1796.

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

Tax lists naming slaveholders and number of enslaved; estate inventories naming enslaved individuals; slave schedules (1850, 1860); manumission records; bills of sale. Kentucky was a slave state that did not secede.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.record_losses: 1873 fire destroyed courthouse and records; some later records also lost in feuds and violence
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Jackson. 1873 fire destroyed courthouse and records. Known historically for Breathitt County feuds (Baker-Howard, Hargis-Cockrell-Callahan) — court records particularly important but many lost. KDLA has some surviving copies. Eastern Kentucky mountains — strong Appalachian oral tradition. Breathitt County Museum. Lees College (now KY Christian Univ.) had local history collection. Jackson city records supplement courthouse losses. FamilySearch microfilm from post-fire restart.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1873 to present. Some gaps due to 1873 fire destroyed courthouse and records; some later records also lost in feuds and violence.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1873 fire destroyed courthouse and records; some later records also lost in feuds and violence

source_profiles.source_name: Breathitt County Coal Mining and Company Records
source_profiles.repository: Southeast KY Community & Technical College / UK Special Collections
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Eastern Kentucky coalfield. Company town records, mine payrolls, commissary records, and United Mine Workers union records. Essential for documenting mining families.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hardinsburg. Records intact from 1799 formation. Ohio River county in western Kentucky. Breckinridge County Historical Society. Cloverport river port records. Lincoln Heritage Scenic Highway. Iron industry furnace records (Tar Spring furnace). FamilySearch microfilm. Check Hardin County for pre-1799 records. Mixed Union/Confederate sympathies during Civil War.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1799 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1799. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

Annual tax lists enumerate heads of household, land acreage, town lots, horses, cattle, and (pre-1865) enslaved persons. Essential substitute when other records destroyed.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Shepherdsville. Formed 1796 from Jefferson and Nelson. Salt River heritage — Bullitt's Lick salt works were among Kentucky's earliest industries. Bullitt County Genealogical Society active. Bernheim Arboretum and Research Forest. Pioneer cemetery records. Near Louisville metro — check Jefferson County for overlapping early records. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1796 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1796. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Morgantown. Formed 1810 from Logan and Ohio. Green River area — Virginia military district land grants. Butler County Historical Society. Morgantown has small-town records well preserved. Rochester (river port) records. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Logan and Ohio counties for pre-1810 ancestors.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1810 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1810. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Princeton. Formed 1809 from Livingston. Western Kentucky. Caldwell County Historical Society. Princeton was a regional center. Adsmore house museum. Night Rider tobacco wars (1905-1909) records document this era of agrarian unrest. FamilySearch microfilm. Check Livingston County for pre-1809 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1809 to present. Continuous coverage.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1809. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Murray. 1842 fire destroyed early records. Formed 1822 from Hickman. Jackson Purchase region (acquired from Chickasaw 1818). Murray State University Pogue Library special collections hold regional materials. Calloway County Historical Society. Between-the-Rivers community (displaced by Land Between the Lakes). FamilySearch microfilm for post-1842 records. KDLA has some surviving early documents.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1842 to present. Some gaps due to 1842 courthouse fire destroyed early records.
Marriage bonds, licenses, and certificates from 1842. Kentucky marriage bonds are particularly valuable — they name the bride, groom, and bondsman (often a relative).

County Court order books contain guardian appointments, apprentice bindings, road orders, slave patrols, tavern licenses, and miscellaneous county business. Essential for genealogical research in Kentucky.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Alexandria. Formed 1794 from Harrison, Mason, and Scott. Northern Kentucky (Greater Cincinnati area). Newport and Dayton city records extensive. Campbell County Historical Society. Newport Barracks military records (1803-1894). German and Irish immigrant communities. Cincinnati-area church records overlap. FamilySearch microfilm. Campbell County Public Library local history room.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1794 to present. Continuous coverage.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Bardwell. Formed 1886 from Ballard — Kentucky's youngest county. Far western Kentucky (Jackson Purchase). Small county with limited institutional resources. Check Ballard County for pre-1886 records. FamilySearch microfilm. Agricultural community — tobacco farming records.
